title: Sanitize legacy instrument error stacks (back-port cleanErrorStack)
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

Source: #1154 review item R16 (datadir/review-1154.md:101-106, 327-330).

## Problem (real, page-observable leak)
The LEGACY js-instrument rethrows errors bare at
`Extension/src/lib/js-instruments.ts:442-452`. Because legacy runs in the page world, any
page-observable `.stack` on those errors leaks the extension UUID (moz-extension://<uuid>/...)
plus the instrument's own wrapper frames — a trivial detection vector.

## Goal (confirmed direction, Stefan)
Catch and remove the extension's own code from error stacks WHERE POSSIBLE before the error
becomes page-observable:
- Back-port stealth's `cleanErrorStack` (strips frames containing `getURL("")`) and hook it at
  the legacy rethrow site (~`js-instruments.ts:451`).
- "Where possible": legacy runs in the page world, so error RECONSTRUCTION is harder than
  stealth's isolated-world path. The stack-CLEANING transfers directly; adapt the error
  reconstruction for the page world as far as feasible.

## Relationships
- Part of the legacy-footprint hardening umbrella.
- Sibling to GH #1204 (channel hardening) and to R24 (stack-frame filtering for attribution).
- Relates to ADR-0001 (`docs/developers/adr/0001-retain-legacy-js-instrument.md`) follow-ups
  and to GH #1187 (relax legacy/stealth mutual exclusion via footprint reduction).
